parsed_review.summary: CP00-648 is a Risk-A, 10-unit descriptor-only RP21 Admin Console "Claude Review Packet" boundary slice (RP21.P02.M09.S03-S12). The pack-level descriptor production-readiness posture is correct and well-supported: manifest production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false with pack_level_claude_review.status=pending_review (this review is the gate), and construction-inspection keeps admin_console_claude_review_packet_boundary_descriptor_verified=false until closeout. Implementation (registry.js bindings/requirements, service.js descriptor+case-set+packet+handoff factories chaining from the CP647 source descriptor, validators.js coverage+descriptor validators) is fully descriptor-only and frozen. The committed contracts/admin-console-contract.json matches the generated projection (generated_from_pack_id=CP00-648, current_pack=CP00-648, historical=[645,646,647], runtime_opened=false, cp648_closeout_handoff→CP00-649/RP21.P02.M09.S13). Tests in packages/admin/test/model.test.js include three dedicated CP648 groups asserting coverage, descriptor-only runtime boundaries, no-write attestation, and handoff. Local validation evidence is green (node --check ×7 exit 0; rp21:admin-console:validate exit 0, which runs without --write so writes_product_state=false holds). No-write/no-real-data is attested and enforced by ~30 falseFlags checks per descriptor. Claude is correctly read-only and NOT final approver (read_only=true; tools Read/Grep/Glob only; promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false enforced; claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false). Hermes patterns are deterministic review-packet/receipt only (runtime_receipt_emitted=false; emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). CP178+ hardening passes: auth preflight passed with non-zero valid-JSON stdout, prompt 2390B<25600 with no full diff/source, no forbidden tools, and no prior valid/invalid review attempts. The pack makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim, so it does not block the CP closeout goal; the human/enterprise-trust authority boundary is correctly held outside this receipt. One non-blocking P3 descriptor-metadata nit noted (CP648 reuses CP647 row-extras, carrying test/golden labels on two review-packet rows). No P0/P1/P2.

findings.title: CP648 reuses CP647 row-extras, leaving test/golden labels on two Claude Review Packet rows
findings.details: ADMIN_CONSOLE_CP648_ROW_EXTRAS is aliased directly to ADMIN_CONSOLE_CP647_ROW_EXTRAS. As a result, the CP648 (micro-phase RP21.P02.M09 'Claude Review Packet') case-set rows primary_happy_path and secondary_workflow_path inherit CP647 'Test And Golden Case Set' descriptor hints: path_descriptor 'admin-console-test-golden-primary'/'admin-console-test-golden-secondary', golden_case_descriptor_only:true, and executes_golden_case_runtime:false (visible in the committed contract around lines 7458-7479). These golden-case labels are semantically a CP647 concept and read slightly off for a Claude Review Packet boundary slice. Impact is cosmetic only: the validators do not assert these label values, and every safety boundary still holds for these rows (descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, raw_payload_included:false, executes_golden_case_runtime:false). No functional, security, coverage, or boundary effect.
findings.recommendation: Optional future cleanup: give CP648 its own ROW_EXTRAS (or override primary_happy_path/secondary_workflow_path) with review-packet-appropriate path descriptors instead of aliasing CP647's golden-case extras. Not required for CP00-648 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Accepted as non-blocking informational note; no action required for CP00-648 closeout. Cosmetic descriptor-metadata only; all descriptor-only/no-runtime/no-write boundaries verified intact.
